meta.title: Art Studio & Showcase Projects | Who's to Say ? Foundation
meta.description: Art, design, and showcase preparation with clear project scope, consent, and service boundaries.

hero.title: Art Studio & Showcase Projects
hero.subtitle: Create, share, and celebrate work in supportive settings — confidence, presentation, and participant-owned creative output first.
hero.bestFor: Creators who light up when people see and appreciate what they make.

blocks.html: Art Studio & Showcase Projects helps creators design, make, and present digital or physical work. Projects may include digital art, photography, crafts, prints, simple products, or showcase displays.

The focus is creative expression, presentation practice, and pride in completed work. Commercial sales, custody of inventory, fundraising use, and public display should be handled only with clear consent and written terms.

items: Design sessions for digital or physical pieces
Simple projects such as stickers, prints, keychains, small crafts, or photo collections
Building a set of items for a future display table, online gallery, or personal portfolio
Learning how to set up a table or display
Practicing how to talk about creative work
Participating in low-pressure showcases or markets when available and appropriate
Understanding that creative work has value
Discussing gifting, display, sale, or donation options only with the right consent
Keeping participant ownership and permission clear
Early exposure to showcase and entrepreneurship concepts

getStarted.title: Get Started
getStarted.prompt: Want to plan a first set of items or showcase goal?

blocks.html: Participants create art, prints, and small projects, then practice sharing them at events and showcases. Any public display, sale, or fundraising use should have a separate consent and ownership record.
